titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Elizabeth Tyrrell 1, September, 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Refers to enclosed sports day program. William Upton Tyrrell was responsible for his unit winning a race.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
1 September 1917 My dear Mamy. The reports went off A1 yesterday
and we all spent the most
enjoyable afternoon we have had
for a long time. I enclose the
progamme and the detail of the
Representative race. Each coy
and head quarters were represented.
Of course C coy or won thought
not too easily so I had the honour
of delivering the winning message
to the C.O which I did by more or
less spitting it at him. Moore
was the Platoon commander who
brought it to me and I just
stuck it in my mouth, while
saddling up and when I got to the
C.O of course could not stop
so I just spit it out passing.
I do not know if that is
treating the Commaning Officer
with proper respect. However my
company won - The horse race
also caused much excitement. Please keep these enclosures
for me as they are very interesting The weather has improved
considerably the last day or
two. It is a shame for the Canon if he
comes home. I suppose there
must have been talk of him
coming out & so he have
old Lindsay a few extra drinks
to clear out. No news but with best love Your most affec Son
Willie
